chain_edges.notes: Hyperscaler-lab capital coupling (Google $40B, Amazon $25B, MS $13B+) is the upstream commitment that the Mag 7 Q1 prints are now stress-tested against for ROI.
Three-cloud frontier-training oligopoly with $100B+ commits sets up the capex-vs-ROI tension that Q1 earnings force a hyperscaler to gate by token-margin floor.
Per-token-margin gating on incremental GPU capex would push hyperscalers further into inference-tuned SKUs (TPU 8i, Trainium-i) to hit $/query economics.
Mag 7 capex-ROI reckoning amplifies the SaaS displacement repricing: the same per-API-call lens redirects valuations across the stack.
5-pole hyperscaler-lab exclusives establish the codesigned compute commitments whose ROI now needs explicit token-margin justification at Q1 prints.
GGUF supply-chain CVE class (SGLang Jinja2 SSTI) feeds the inference-server CVE sub-catalog rationale.
Agent-in-the-loop secret exfiltration via CI prompt-injection chain is part of the 6-CVE stack motivating the sub-catalog.
MCP-protocol attack surface and KEV uplift reinforce a dedicated AI-Infra CVE category beyond OWASP MCP Top 10.
Earlier LLM-INFRA-CWE/AI-Infra tag prediction is the narrower precursor; this prediction extends scope to a regulatory primitive.
Agent Control Plane convergence (Okta/Entra agent-IDs as first-class) supplies the technical primitive that statutory agent-identity registration codifies.
MCPwn-driven OWASP MCP Top 10 + MCP Gateway industry standard underpins the MCP-call-chain logging requirement in the predicted statute.
AI-extension OAuth scope category at SSO providers is a building block for per-action consent in the predicted state law.
Statutory agent-identity push and AI-Infra-CVE regulatory primitive both flow from the same Federal Register RFI + state-level pull-forward.

relations.notes: Both diagnose the hyperscaler AI-capex regime: one focuses on funding-origin lock-in, the other on ROI gating; complementary facets.
Frontier capacity capture and per-token-margin gating describe complementary halves of the hyperscaler training-capex thesis.
Training-vs-inference SKU split and per-token-margin gating are mutually reinforcing facets of the same capex-discipline shift.
5-pole hyperscaler-lab exclusives and AI-capex ROI gating are independent facets of the same hyperscaler compute-commitment regime.
Per-token-margin floor on GPU capex and Robot-as-a-Service per-robot cloud-compute charges share the same recurring-revenue ROI logic.
Q4 sub-catalog graduation entails the Q3 AI-Infra CVE-tag step; this prediction is the stronger downstream claim.
GGUF supply-chain hardening and AI-Infra sub-catalog target overlapping CVE classes via different actors.
MCP protocol-layer hardening and AI-Infra CVE sub-catalog are parallel regulatory tracks against the same agent-runtime threat surface.
Agent-in-the-loop secret exfiltration sits in CIS/OWASP risk lists while AI-Infra sub-catalog sits in CISA/NIST; complementary venues.
Both extend AI security baselines (CVE sub-catalog vs third-party-vendor + URL-naming); different control planes.
Agent control-plane governance and AI-Infra CVE sub-catalog are complementary enterprise-control responses to agent runtime risk.
Statutory agent-identity (regulatory layer) and Agent Control Plane (technical layer) are independent but mutually reinforcing facets of the same agent-governance theme.
MCP protocol hardening (OWASP MCP Top 10, MCP-over-mTLS) and statutory agent-identity registration address overlapping audit needs from different angles.
Both predict 2026 regulatory codification triggered by the same public-opinion + incident corpus; one targets agent-identity, the other AI-Infra CVEs.
AI-extension OAuth scope governance and statutory per-action consent target overlapping consent-and-audit terrain across SSO and state-law layers.
Third-party-vendor security baseline and agent-identity statutes both raise compliance floors for agent-touching infrastructure.
